title: Go Data Types
content:
### Basic Data Types

Go is statically typed - every variable has a known type at compile time.

### Numeric Types

| Type | Description | Size |
|------|-------------|------|
| `int` | Platform-dependent | 32 or 64 bit |
| `int8` | Signed 8-bit | -128 to 127 |
| `int16` | Signed 16-bit | -32768 to 32767 |
| `int32` | Signed 32-bit | -2^31 to 2^31-1 |
| `int64` | Signed 64-bit | -2^63 to 2^63-1 |
| `uint` | Unsigned platform-dependent | 32 or 64 bit |
| `uint8` | Unsigned 8-bit (byte) | 0 to 255 |
| `uint16` | Unsigned 16-bit | 0 to 65535 |
| `uint32` | Unsigned 32-bit | 0 to 2^32-1 |
| `uint64` | Unsigned 64-bit | 0 to 2^64-1 |
| `float32` | IEEE-754 32-bit | ~6 decimal digits |
| `float64` | IEEE-754 64-bit | ~15 decimal digits |
| `complex64` | Complex with float32 parts | real + imag |
| `complex128` | Complex with float64 parts | real + imag |

### Other Basic Types

```go
bool        // true or false
string      // sequence of bytes (UTF-8)
error       // built-in interface for errors
byte        // alias for uint8
rune        // alias for int32 (Unicode code point)
```

### Composite Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `array` | Fixed-length sequence |
| `slice` | Variable-length sequence |
| `struct` | Collection of fields |
| `map` | Key-value pairs |
| `pointer` | Memory address |
| `function` | First-class functions |
| `interface` | Method sets |
| `channel` | Communication |

> **Key Point:** `int` and `uint` are platform-dependent (32-bit on 32-bit systems, 64-bit on 64-bit systems). Use explicit sizes for portability.
codeExample:
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "math"
)

func main() {
    var i int = 42
    var f float64 = 3.14
    var b bool = true
    var s string = "hello"
    var r rune = 'A'

    fmt.Printf("int: %d\n", i)
    fmt.Printf("float64: %f\n", f)
    fmt.Printf("bool: %t\n", b)
    fmt.Printf("string: %s\n", s)
    fmt.Printf("rune: %c\n", r)
    fmt.Printf("max int64: %d\n", math.MaxInt64)
}

quiz.question: Which type represents a Unicode code point?
